_comment: TEMPLATE, not a live allowlist -- tools/escape_check.py reads
tools/escape_allowlist.json (no '.template' in the name), which does
NOT ship with this toolkit by default (same convention as
gateway/config.template.yaml -> gateway/config.yaml).
To use this mechanism: copy this file to escape_allowlist.json next
to it, delete this '_comment' key, and replace the one example entry
below with your own real escape/concession clause(s).
Each entry hash-pins ONE permanent escape/concession clause in a
carrier document (e.g. CLAUDE.md) to the decision-log section that
authorizes it, so a silent drift between the two is caught
mechanically. Fields:
id             short, unique, human-readable slug for this entry.
carrier_file   path (repo-root-relative) to the file quoting the
clause verbatim.
carrier_anchor a distinctive substring of the clause, copied
verbatim from carrier_file (whitespace-folded before
comparison, so a markdown reflow does not break it).
decision_id    the D-NNNN id of the decision that authorizes this
clause (must match ^D-\d{4}$).
decision_file  path to the VERBOSE, section-headed decision log
that carries this id as a '## D-NNNN[ -- title]'
heading. NOTE: this toolkit's own DECISIONS.md is a
terse one-line-per-decision index by design and
does NOT have such headings -- this mechanism needs
a verbose decision document of its own before any
real entry can be added (see tools/escape_check.py's
DEFAULT_DECISION_FILE_REL comment for the same
point). Point decision_file at whatever that
document ends up being for your deployment.
section_sha256 sha256 hex digest of the decision section, computed
by 'python tools/escape_check.py --hash D-NNNN'
once decision_file exists and has that section.
affirmed       YYYY-MM-DD the entry was last reviewed/re-affirmed.
note           optional free-text context for a future reader.

entries.carrier_anchor: replace this with a verbatim substring of your real clause
entries.note: placeholder example -- this entry FAILS by design (D-0000/docs/DECISIONS_FULL.md do not exist) until replaced with real values, so running escape_check.py against an un-edited copy of this template fails loudly instead of silently reporting a fake OK
